description: Binary assertions for the install/upgrade/uninstall experience. Validates that a user on a clean machine can go from zero to productive with minimal friction.
test_prompt: You are testing the skippy-agentspace installation experience. Simulate being a user who just cloned the repo to a clean machine with only git, bash, and jq installed. Walk through the complete install, verify, uninstall, and reinstall cycle. For each step, describe exactly what happens, what output the user sees, and what could go wrong. Do NOT actually execute anything -- just output every step, every decision point, every error message, and every edge case you'd encounter.

assertions.assertion: Running install.sh with no args shows a status/help message, not an error
assertions.check: First-time user should see what skills are available, not a cryptic error

assertions.assertion: The install process does NOT require Node.js, Python, or bun as prerequisites
assertions.check: Shell-only dependency chain -- bash + git + jq should be enough

assertions.assertion: install.sh --all works and installs all 24 skills in under 10 seconds
assertions.check: Copy operations should be fast -- no network, no builds

assertions.assertion: After install, /clear in Claude Code picks up the new skills immediately
assertions.check: No manual restart or session refresh required beyond /clear

assertions.assertion: Installing a non-existent skill gives a clear error naming the skill and suggesting alternatives
assertions.check: Not just 'not found' -- tell the user what IS available

assertions.assertion: Installing to a path without write permissions gives a clear error with fix instructions
assertions.check: Don't just fail silently -- tell user to fix permissions or use sudo

assertions.assertion: Running install.sh outside the repo directory gives a clear error
assertions.check: Detect when not in repo root and tell the user to cd

assertions.assertion: Re-installing over existing skills backs up the old version first
assertions.check: Never silently overwrite -- always preserve the user's customizations

assertions.assertion: The upgrade path preserves user customizations to SKILL.md files
assertions.check: If user edited a skill, don't clobber their changes without warning

assertions.assertion: uninstall.sh removes ALL traces of installed skills (both symlinks AND copied dirs)
assertions.check: Known bug: uninstall only checks symlinks, not copies. Must be fixed.

assertions.assertion: uninstall.sh is idempotent -- running twice produces no errors
assertions.check: Second run should say 'already uninstalled' or similar, not crash

assertions.assertion: Uninstalling a single skill does not affect other installed skills
Install/uninstall output uses color coding (green=success, red=error, yellow=warning)
assertions.check: Visual feedback helps users scan output quickly

assertions.assertion: Install summary shows: count installed, count skipped, count failed
assertions.check: User should know exactly what happened without reading the full log

assertions.assertion: install.sh works on both macOS (zsh default) and Linux (bash default)
assertions.check: No macOS-specific flags (like BSD sed) or Linux-specific assumptions

assertions.assertion: install.sh detects and handles the PAI symlink architecture (skills/ -> ~/.config/pai/Skills/)
assertions.check: Should follow the symlink and install to the real target
